description: Fix Retry-After header parsing in the Discord plugin to use the SDK's parseRetryAfterMs utility instead of reimplementing it incorrectly.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/discord && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Use SDK parseRetryAfterMs in Discord API caller
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the Discord plugin to correctly parse Retry-After headers in both seconds and HTTP-date formats so that AI agents receive valid retry timing metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Line ~232 in discord-api.ts uses parseRetryAfterMs from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk instead of `Number(retryAfterHeader) * 1000`
The import for parseRetryAfterMs is added to the existing @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk import line
The behavior for numeric Retry-After values is unchanged
HTTP-date format Retry-After values are correctly parsed instead of producing NaN
The secondary retry_after parsing from the JSON body (lines 238-245) is preserved — it takes precedence when present
cd plugins/discord && npm run build passes
cd plugins/discord && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/discord && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/discord && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: plugins/discord/src/discord-api.ts

The bug is on line 232:
```
const retryMs = retryAfterHeader ? Number(retryAfterHeader) * 1000 : undefined;
```

Replace with:
```
const retryMs = retryAfterHeader !== null ? parseRetryAfterMs(retryAfterHeader) : undefined;
```

Add `parseRetryAfterMs` to the existing import from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk' at line 1.

IMPORTANT: The Discord API also returns `retry_after` in the JSON response body (lines 238-245). That secondary parsing must be preserved — it overrides the header value when present. Only the header parsing (line 232) needs to change.
